Text: ramadan day 5. the hardest days are 3-5. after this your body adjusts and the fast gets easier. push through today.
4 pages after each salah = complete the quran in 30 days. that's 20 pages per day spread across 5 prayers. doesn't feel overwhelming when you break it down.
the best dua is the one you make at iftar when you're exhausted and your heart is completely open. don't waste that window scrolling.

built a free ramadan tracker app because i needed one that works offline and doesn't need an account. fasting, prayers, quran progress, tasbih, taraweeh. your data stays on your device. ramadan-tracker.surge.sh
your tongue is fasting too. no backbiting, no gossip, no harsh words. the stomach fast is easy mode. the tongue fast is where the real discipline lives.
suhoor tip that changed my ramadan: slow-release carbs (oats, whole grain bread) + protein + water. no fried food. the difference at 3pm is night and day.
that 2am tahajjud prayer hits different when you're already awake for suhoor. the house is quiet. just you and your creator. don't skip it.
been using prayerlock to track my prayer streaks this ramadan. 5 days, 25 prayers logged. the streak counter is surprisingly motivating. prayerlock.surge.sh
5 days in and the phone screen time is already dropping. ramadan strips away the noise whether you planned for it or not.
honest question: what's the hardest prayer to maintain during ramadan for you? for me it's fajr. and it's supposedly the most rewarded one.
the sahaba prepared for ramadan 6 months in advance and celebrated it for 6 months after. we can barely commit for 30 days. perspective.
one week of ramadan done. if you've been consistent with all 5 prayers, that's 35 prayers you showed up for. don't break the chain now.
iftar with family is one of those things you don't appreciate until you can't do it anymore. be present tonight. phones away.
the quran was revealed in ramadan. so every page you read this month carries extra weight. 4 pages after fajr. 4 after dhuhr. 4 after asr. 4 after maghrib. 4 after isha. done in 30 days.
tracking shows the hardest prayer to maintain during ramadan is fajr. and it's the most rewarded one. set 3 alarms if you have to.
the streak counter in my prayer tracker changed everything. seeing '14 days consecutive fajr' makes you not want to break it. gamification works on ibadah too.
ramadan week 1 recap: how many days have you completed all 5 prayers? no judgment. just checking in. accountability helps.
what's the one habit from ramadan you actually kept after last year? for me it was the quran reading. lost the tahajjud by week 2 of shawwal though.
if you haven't started reading quran yet this ramadan, start today. 4 pages after each salah. simple math. don't let perfect be the enemy of good.
feature request from a friend: add sadaqah tracking to the ramadan app. how much you gave, when, where. makes you more intentional about charity. adding it this week.
